title: Running 128 Coding Agents at Once
summary: Cursor's Sam Whitmore discusses scaling coding agents with Baseten representatives. The conversation covers running hundreds of agents in parallel, enabling agents to communicate and review each other's work, and transitioning from task parallelization to building integrated multi-agent systems.
writeup: Cursor's Sam Whitmore talks with Baseten representatives about scaling coding agents to run hundreds in parallel. The discussion covers enabling agents to communicate and review each other's work, and it traces the shift from simple task parallelization to integrated multi-agent systems. The core takeaway is that orchestration, not just raw parallelism, is what turns many concurrent agents into a coordinated system.
